_x000d_

rows.Title: SIM card not recognized
rows.Problem: Symptoms: No power. Press Power Key -> Booting OK but CP Binary shows. Power on with JIG -> Initially consumes 200mA to 2.7A current. Analysis: Physical inspection shows no damage. MAIN PBA swap test shows defect. Main PBA analysis: CP_PS_HOLD goes low. VDD_CP short to GND [DCR:1.5]. Apply 1V at VDD_CP using DC cable, hot spot seen over U12022(CP). U12022(CP) issue suspected.
rows.Ai Summary: The phone shows no power and fails to recognize the SIM card, even after booting, suggesting a hardware issue with the main processor board.
rows.Severity Reason: The device fails to power on properly and shows no response to power key, preventing any basic operation such as SIM recognition or booting.

rows.Title: Device automatically turns off when playing songs on YouTube

rows.Title: Vibration or echo sound observed via rear cover during MO / MT call
rows.Problem: In Galaxy A26 (SM-A266B), customer reported vibration or echo sound observed via rear cover during MO / MT call. Vibration is observed near rear camera but has not been turned on by users. Internal testing confirms the issue and has been replicated in four field units. Similar issue previously reported for Galaxy M55 and F55.
rows.Ai Summary: Users hear vibration or echo sounds through the rear cover during phone calls, even when vibration is off.
rows.Severity Reason: The echo or vibration during calls interferes with call clarity and user experience, especially in noisy environments, but does not prevent device operation or communication.

rows.Title: Rear camera sluggish
rows.Problem: Camera image is getting flicker / shuttered in rear camera preview mode. Issue reproduction steps: Run camera application >> Select rear camera >> Start recording a video --> Now move the camera from one direction to another and observed. Expectations: No such issue occurred while capturing a video via camera application.
rows.Ai Summary: The rear camera preview flickers and shudders when the user moves the phone during video recording.
rows.Severity Reason: The issue affects the usability of the rear camera during live preview and video recording, making it difficult to capture stable footage, but the device remains otherwise functional.

rows.Title: The selected wallpaper in call background doesn't show up

rows.Severity Reason: The issue affects device identification and may impact service access or diagnostics, but the device remains otherwise functional and usable.

rows.Title: AOD turns on automatically when power saver is turned on through quick panel.
rows.Problem: Since updating my Samsung Galaxy S21 FE to One UI 7, I’ve encountered two issues: Power Saving Mode Not Working: When I enable Power Saving Mode (via Settings > Battery > Power Saving or Quick Settings toggle), it does not reduce battery consumption or limit background activities as expected. Features like CPU speed reduction, screen brightness adjustment, or app restrictions do not seem to activate. The battery drains at the same rate as when Power Saving Mode is off. Always On Display (AOD) Automatically Turning On: The AOD turns on randomly, even when I have it set to "Tap to show" or disabled in Settings > Lock Screen > Always On Display. This happens multiple  a day, regardless of whether the phone is in use or idle, and it drains the battery unnecessarily

rows.Title: Issues with camera stabilization
rows.Problem: Dear Samsung Support Team, I am writing to report a serious issue with my Samsung Galaxy A55, which I purchased for ₹47,000 — primarily because of its camera performance, including the optical image stabilization (OIS) feature. However, after updating my phone to Android 15, I have noticed a significant decline in camera stabilization quality. The videos and photos are now much shakier than before, and the camera no longer performs the way it did when I bought it. This has been extremely disappointing, as I invested in this device specifically for its camera capabilities. I have already tried clearing the camera cache, restarting the phone, but the issue persists. It seems to be directly linked to the recent software update. I trusted Samsung's quality when making this purchase, and I hope this issue can be resolved quickly to restore my confidence in the brand. Looking forward to your response Aditya Bhatt
rows.Ai Summary: The camera now produces shakier videos and photos after the Android 15 update, significantly reducing the quality of images and videos.

rows.Severity Reason: Users cannot use fingerprint authentication to unlock the device, which is a core security and convenience feature, but the device remains otherwise functional.

rows.Title: Blur effect at the bottom of YT's nav bar is missing
rows.Problem: The blur effect on the bottom navigation bar in the YouTube app is gone. It used to have a frosted-glass blur behind the Home, Shorts, and Library buttons, which looked very rich. Now it’s just a solid color with no blur or transparency. Power saving mode is OFF, and I’ve tried everything—disabling hardware overlays, clearing app data, restarting the phone, even rolling back to older YouTube versions—but nothing brings the blur back. The blur used to appear even on older One UI versions, so this seems like a recent UI rendering issue. Please look into this and restore the visual blur effect, or provide an option to re-enable it. Thank you!
rows.Ai Summary: The YouTube app no longer shows the blur effect on its navigation bar, making the interface look flat and less visually appealing.
rows.Severity Reason: The issue affects the visual experience of the app but does not prevent users from using YouTube or accessing content.

rows.Title: Rotate to Landscape mode showing disable in device

I am writing to report an issue I am facing on my Samsung Galaxy A15 5G device regarding the screen rotation functionality. Specifically, I am unable to use the landscape mode on both the home screen and the app drawer. I have noticed that the toggle for enabling landscape mode appears to be greyed out and unresponsive. No matter how many  I try, I am unable to turn it on. This prevents me from using my phone in landscape mode, which is essential for my user experience. To help you understand the issue better, I have also attached a screen recording/video that clearly shows the problem in action. I would really appreciate it if you could look into this matter and provide guidance on how to resolve it. If it is a known bug or a software glitch, kindly let me know if an up expected soon. Thank you ,

rows.Title: Charging error message pop up
rows.Problem: Error message pop up as "Slow charging. Make sure charger is connected and not damaged" after connecting a charger to a device. NOTE: In another model same charger is working fine. Upon inspection, inserting the charger occasionally triggered an error, causing the battery percentage to display 0%. Removing the charger restored the battery percentage to its original level. We have also tested another A166 handset, and the same issue persists.
rows.Ai Summary: The device shows a charging error message and the battery reads 0% when a charger is connected, which disrupts normal charging and usage.
rows.Severity Reason: The error message and battery display issue prevent reliable charging, affecting daily use, but the device remains otherwise functional and can be restored by removing the charger.

rows.Title: Speaker volume is perceived as diminished during the ringing phase of an outgoing call

Place a call to any recipient 3. Activate the speakerphone function and observe the speaker audio is low during the call's ringing phase. Note: This issue was not present in the previous Fold 6 model. The issue is not present once the call is answered. Expected Behavior: The speaker volume should remain consistent throughout the call, including the outgoing ringing phase.
rows.Ai Summary: The speaker sounds much quieter during the ringing phase of an outgoing call, making it hard to hear the ringtone or respond to the call.
rows.Severity Reason: This issue affects call initiation experience, making it difficult for users to detect incoming calls, but does not prevent the device from functioning or using other features.

rows.Title: Network issue with dual SIM cards
rows.Problem: Intermittent network connectivity observed in one of the SIM slots when two active SIM cards are inserted simultaneously. When both SIM slots are populated with active SIM cards (tested with Jio-Jio, Jio-Airtel, and Airtel-Vi combinations), one of the SIMs fails to register with the network. This issue persists despite verification by an MSC engineer and replacement of the PBA, Sub PBA, and FRC cable. A case has been logged by Manish Kumar. Reproduction Steps: Insert two active operator SIM cards into both SIM slots. Observe the network status of each SIM slot. Observed Result: One of the SIM slots consistently fails to connect or maintain a stable connection to its respective network. Expected Result: Both active SIM cards are expected to latch onto their respective networks and provide stable connectivity when inserted in a dual-SIM enabled device.
rows.Ai Summary: One of the two SIM cards fails to connect to the network when both are active, even after hardware checks and replacements.
rows.Severity Reason: The issue prevents reliable use of one SIM card in a dual-SIM setup, which impacts users' ability to switch between networks or maintain consistent connectivity, but the device remains otherwise functional.

rows.Title: The Up Volume Key on the device is non-functional

Expected Result: The Up Volume Key should function normally after the ELS process, allowing the user to increase the device's volume as intended.
rows.Ai Summary: The volume up button doesn't work and can't increase the device's volume.
rows.Severity Reason: Users cannot adjust volume using the physical volume key, which impacts basic device functionality during calls or media playback.

rows.Severity Reason: The device experiences repeated soft reboots and loss of core functionalities like fingerprint unlock and navigation, which prevents normal use of the device.

rows.Title: Issue observed with mobile hotspot
rows.Problem:
Issue_x000d_
Intermittent performance degradation observed with mobile hotspot functionality when connected to a secondary device. This manifests as:_x000d_
1. Reduced data transmission speed_x000d_
2. Unscheduled disconnection of the mobile hotspot, followed by automatic reconnection within 2-3 minutes_x000d_
Note: These issues were consistently observed with both WPA2 and WPA3 mobile hotspot security configurations_x000d_
_x000d_
Reproduction Steps_x000d_
1. Enable and configure the mobile hotspot on the primary device_x000d_
2. Connect a secondary device to the configured hotspot_x000d_
Initiate video streaming via the YouTube application on the connected device for a duration of 15-20 minutes_x000d_
Observe for the intermittent occurrence of the aforementioned issues_x000d_
_x000d_
Expectations_x000d_
Consistent and stable mobile hotspot connectivity with reliable data speeds and without unexpected disconnections is expected.

rows.Title: IMEI information remains visible after device lock

rows.Title: Handset Auto Restart while handset going to Sleep Mode
rows.Problem: Handset Auto Restart while handset going to Sleep Mode. Precondition: Handset Operating through NTF Dobot Machine. Defect Sample: 1ea. Reproducibility Rate: Always (01/01). Occurrence Steps: Run Python Script [Performance Test] in NTF Dobot Machine to Operate handset --> Handset Auto Restart while handset going to Sleep Mode. Note: Video attached. Feedback by: Manish Kumar. Expected Result: Abnormality should not occurrence
rows.Ai Summary: The phone automatically restarts when going to sleep mode during performance testing.
rows.Severity Reason: The device restarts unexpectedly during normal operation, preventing users from using the phone reliably and potentially causing data loss or disruption in usage.

rows.Title: No network service
rows.Problem: No network service on the handset persists even after replacing the motherboard (PBA) and SUB-PBA. Issue observed with all operator SIM cards. As per govt website Ceir.gov.in, both are valid and keystring (*#0011#) does not show GMM value and says app as UNBLOCK. Expectations: Please investigate and resolve the issue using the attached log file and video.
rows.Ai Summary: The phone shows no network service even after hardware replacement and with all SIM cards, making it impossible to connect to any network.
rows.Severity Reason: The device cannot access any network service, which prevents basic functions like calls, messages, or internet access, rendering the device unusable for communication.

rows.Title: Network issues with eSIM and physical SIM in the second slot
rows.Problem: Handset SM-S931B has network issues with eSIM and physical SIM in the second slot. eSIM shows "Turn On" but remains in loading state. Physical SIM in the second slot does not detect the SIM card. Factory reset did not resolve the issue. eSIM converted to physical SIM and tested on another device with no such issue observed. Expectation: eSIM should be removed and physical SIM card should be accepted by the device.
rows.Ai Summary: The phone fails to properly detect or activate both eSIM and physical SIM cards in the second slot, making it impossible to use either SIM for network connectivity.
rows.Severity Reason: Users cannot use either SIM for calls or data, which significantly limits functionality, but the device remains otherwise usable and can operate with one SIM if manually switched.

rows.Title: Camera Issue
rows.Problem:
Incident Description_x000d_
The device exhibits significant display artifacts and severe animation lag (jerkiness) when transitioning back to the Home Screen or performing other exit procedures after enabling and using the camera's High Dynamic Range (HDR) mode_x000d_
Case ref. by Shiju K S Qup_x000d_
_x000d_
Conditions and Scope_x000d_
The defect is highly conditional and localized:_x000d_
_x000d_
Affected Display: The issue is exclusively isolated to the Sub-Display_x000d_
_x000d_
Trigger Condition: The malfunction occurs only when the Camera's HDR mode is enabled_x000d_
_x000d_
Normal Operation: When the HDR mode is explicitly disabled, the Sub-Display functions correctly, and screen transitions are smooth_x000d_
_x000d_
Main Display Status: The Main Display operates without any display artifacts or performance degradation, regardless of whether the HDR mode is enabled or disabled

rows.Title: Wi-Fi Calling not working
rows.Problem: Wi-Fi Calling Not Working. Since the One UI 8 update, the Wi-Fi Calling feature has completely failed. It will not activate, or it drops calls/fails to connect even when I have a strong Wi-Fi signal. This feature was working perfectly before the update. rows.Title: Contrast and saturation changes drastically in video after update
rows.Problem: Since the UI 7 update, video quality has significantly decreased. I am disappointed. The contrast and saturation in videos change drastically. After UI 8, I also experience object sensing problems in portrait mode. The portrait sensor and edge detection have become disappointing. In photos, the contrast change is very clear. For example, in two photos of my car taken in 2x and 3x portrait modes, the difference is noticeable. In photos of a grey car with number 72, buildings in the background are clearly visible, but only the background near the car is blurred, while the rest of the photo appears clear. I expect better quality from a flagship phone—even ₹20k phones now have better portrait photos. Please fix this Samsung team. I will be very happy if these issues are resolved quickly.
rows.Ai Summary: Video and portrait photos now have poor contrast and saturation, with noticeable quality drops and object sensing issues.

rows.Title: Random log files getting generated and stored automatically
rows.Problem: I noticed today that some sort of log files are getting generated and stored in the "Documents" folder (as long as it's present in the storage) automatically. Initially I saw a large file around 600 MB which I deleted from my phone. Even on deleting the files, they reappear. There is a base file named "release0721_S.LSI.txt" and then periodic "ectsavedata_1min.txt" and "ectsavedata_10min.txt". The base file gets appended with data and keeps increasing in size. So the file I had deleted initially must have been collecting days or even weeks. I'm not exactly sure when this issue started. I found some mentions on reddit about other users facing similar issue on their Samsung Galaxy A55 after the One UI 8 (Android 16) update which I also received a couple weeks ago. I'm also attaching screenshots of the recent files.

rows.Title: Problem faced while giving the directional input
rows.Problem: The issue where a controller (particularly a Bluetooth one) experiences problems, often losing directional input or fully disconnecting, when the phone screen turns off or goes to sleep while using Samsung DeX mode. This issue has been widely reported by users, especially after certain One UI software up (like One UI 8.0 on some devices). The most frequent reports indicate that when the phone's display sleeps, the controller's analog sticks and D-pad stop working, while buttons (A, B, X, Y, shoulder buttons) may continue to function intermittently or be unresponsive. The community consensus is that this is a software bug introduced in a recent One UI up specifically affecting how DeX manages Bluetooth devices when the host phone's display is powered down. It did not affect older versions of DeX.
rows.Ai Summary: Bluetooth controllers lose directional input when the phone screen turns off during Samsung DeX mode, making navigation difficult.
rows.Severity Reason: Users cannot effectively control apps in DeX mode when the screen is off, significantly impacting productivity and usability.

rows.Title: All lock screen widgets disappear when device is plugged to charging
rows.Problem: I am using a Samsung Galaxy S24 FE, and I’ve recently noticed an intermittent issue while charging. Whenever I plug in my phone to charge, the screen automatically turns on, and the lock screen behaves abnormally — the icons and widgets disappear, leaving the screen mostly blank. However, when I unplug the charger, everything instantly returns to normal. Charging itself works properly, and the battery percentage increases as expected. This problem occurs randomly. I’ve tried restarting the device, checking lock screen settings, and using different chargers and cables, but the issue still reappears occasionally. I’d like to understand whether this is a known software bug in One UI or if it could be caused by any app, lock screen setting, or system glitch — and what steps I can take to fix it permanently
rows.Ai Summary: The lock screen widgets vanish when charging and return when unplugged, disrupting access to important app shortcuts.
rows.Severity Reason: While the device remains functional and can be used after unplugging, the loss of widgets impedes quick access to frequently used apps, significantly impacting daily usability.

rows.Title: Adaptive clock stops working when modes are turned on
rows.Problem: The Adaptive Clock feature in One UI 8 stops working whenever the system changes Modes. As soon as a Mode activates (such as Sleep, Work, or Driving Mode), the Adaptive Clock no longer updates or switches wallpapers. Even after the Mode ends, the clock remains static and does not resume functioning automatically. To make it work again, the user must manually open Wallpaper & Style settings and re-apply the wallpaper. This issue occurs every time and is reproducible. Expected behavior is that the Adaptive Clock should continue functioning normally during and after Mode activation or deactivation. Device and software: Galaxy S21 FE (Exynos), One UI 8 with latest patches installed.
rows.Ai Summary: The adaptive clock stops working when system modes like Sleep or Work are activated and doesn't resume automatically, requiring manual intervention to fix.
rows.Severity Reason: The issue affects a key feature of the lock screen that dynamically adjusts to user context, but the device remains otherwise usable. Users must manually reconfigure settings to restore functionality, which impacts daily use but does not prevent overall device operation.

rows.Title: Night mode image quality is not good
rows.Problem: Dear Samsung Camera Team, I am writing to share feedback on the night photography performance of my Samsung smartphone, based on repeated real-world usage. In low-light conditions, I frequently experience noticeable motion blur, especially when photographing people. As shown in the attached examples: The first photo, taken with direct light, appears sharp and acceptable. The second photo, taken in low light without direct illumination, shows heavy blur even though the subject was standing still. This suggests that the camera often uses very slow shutter speeds in night or auto mode, without sufficiently compensating for subject movement. As a result, human subjects are often blurred, while the background may remain relatively detailed. I would strongly recommend: Better motion detection for human subjects in low light. Faster shutter priority or automatic shutter limits when people are detected. Improved Night Mode processing to reduce subject blur, not just increase brightness. Option
rows.Ai Summary: Night mode photos are blurry when taking pictures of people in low light, making them hard to see or use.
rows.Severity Reason: The issue affects a key photography feature in low-light conditions, impacting user experience but does not prevent overall device use.

rows.Title: S23 FE is draining battery at an unacceptable rate.
rows.Problem: My Galaxy S23 FE is draining battery at an unacceptable rate. As per the attached stats, 65% battery was lost in 7h 29m with only 2h 39m of Screen-On- which means the phone cannot even cross 4 hours of SOT on basic usage. This makes the device almost useless for daily life, because I would have to carry a charger everywhere just to keep it alive. During this  only used Instagram and YouTube, no gaming, no navigation, no heavy tasks, yet the battery still drops this fast. I have already done everything from my side: Power Saving Mode ON, 4G only, 60Hz refresh rate, Location OFF. Even after all this, the battery performance is still terrible. Do not suggest up or basic troubleshooting. My phone is already up to  correctly configured. This is clearly a system-level battery optimization failure that Samsung needs to fix. This should be treated as a critical defect, not a minor issue.

rows.Title: Bixby Offline Commands Broken After Update
rows.Problem: Bixby no longer works offline for basic tasks like making calls or turning system features on/off. This functionality worked properly before and has now been broken or silently removed. This is not a small issue. It is a clear feature regression. Offline voice control was one of Bixby’s core advantages. Downgrading it through an update warning is unacceptable and damages user trust. The issue persists despite enabling offline processing, checking language downloads, clearing cache/data, and restarting—so this is not user error. If Samsung is moving Bixby toward cloud-only operation, users deserve clear disclosure, not broken features. If this is a bug, it must be fixed urgently. Please escalate this to the Bixby/One UI team and provide either: a fix restoring offline functionality, or an official statement confirming whether offline commands are being discontinued. Fix it ASAP
rows.Ai Summary: Bixby no longer works offline for basic tasks like making calls or turning on system features, even after trying to fix it.
rows.Severity Reason: Users lose a core functionality (offline voice control) that was previously reliable, impacting daily use and reducing trust in the device's features.

rows.Title: Multiple blank YouTube notifications stack in the notification panel.
rows.Problem: YouTube subion notifications appearing as blank cards in notification panel (One UI 8.0) After updating to One UI 8.0 and using the latest version of the YouTube app, YouTube subion notifications appear in the notification panel as blank notification cards. These notifications only display the YouTube icon and the  1:02 PM, 1:11 PM, 2:00 PM) but no notification text, title, or content. When long-pressing these blank notifications, the system redirects to the YouTube “Subions” notification category, confirming that these are subion alerts. However, the content fails to render in the notification shade. Multiple blank YouTube notifications stack in the notification panel. No text/content is visible, only the app icon and  Clearing YouTube data, reinstalling the app, resetting notification settings does not fix the issue. The issue does not occur when using an older version of the YouTube app.

rows.Title: Back gesture / navigation gesture not working properly (stuck/loop) on Samsung Galaxy S25 Ultra
rows.Problem: I’m experiencing issues while navigating on my Samsung Galaxy S25 Ultra using gesture navigation. When I try to go back in the app by swiping quickly from the left or right edge of the screen, the gesture gets stuck or feels like it goes into a loop. It does not return properly to the camera screen from the chat screen. When the keyboard is open and I try to swipe back to close the keyboard, it only works if I swipe from the edge area above the keyboard. If I swipe back directly from the keyboard area, the phone interprets it as glide typing instead of a back gesture, so the keyboard does not close. This makes it difficult to use one-handed because I have to stretch my thumb above the keyboard to close it. This issue happens frequently during regular usage.
rows.Ai Summary: Navigating with gestures on the phone is frustrating because swipes get stuck or don’t work as expected, making it hard to use the phone one-handed.
rows.Severity Reason: The issue affects core navigation functionality and makes daily use difficult, especially with one-handed operation, but the device remains otherwise functional.

rows.Title: The last-used app often does not appear in the Recent Apps
rows.Problem: The last-used app often does not appear in the Recent Apps screen, even though it is still running in the background. Expected Behavior: Recently used apps should always be visible in the Recent Apps list for quick switching. Actual Behavior: After using an app and navigating away (via gestures, Recent Apps, or Home), the last-used app is removed from the Recent Apps list without user action. The app is not closed or restarted—when opened again from the app drawer, it resumes from the same state. Steps to reproduce: Open multiple apps (e.g., App A, B, C). Use App A last. Switch to another app or go Home. Open Recent Apps. Result: App A is missing from Recent Apps but continues running in the background. Workaround: Restarting the phone temporarily resolves the issue, but it reoccurs frequently.
rows.Ai Summary: Users can't see the last app they used in the Recent Apps list, even though the app is still running.
rows.Severity Reason: This issue prevents users from quickly switching back to recently used apps, which is a common and expected feature for usability.
